prompts:
yes we need to fix ALL of that :D If we could change the claude chat history path names that would be incredible?                                                                                          

⏺ Yes — the chat history is the big one. When you rename the dir, Claude Code's per-project history (keyed by a sanitized version of the absolute path) gets orphaned. Here's the full prompt. Hand it to an
  agent rooted at projects/common/ or higher.

  ---
  Rename the project "protocol2" to "titancall" everywhere, including Claude Code's own
  per-project state. Be careful — several of these are global files; back them up before editing.

  CONTEXT — "titancall" is a working tool (a Titanfall/Portal voice notification router). Its repo is at:
    /Users/d.edens/lab/madness_interactive/projects/common/protocol2
  The dir was named "protocol2" but the project is "titancall". Rename the directory AND fix every
  reference to the old path across the repo, the tool's own config, and Claude Code's state. 

  === A. The repo ===
  1. Move the directory: protocol2 -> titancall (git tracks it). Commit the rename.
     Repo contents: bin/titancall, README.md, install.sh, install-hooks.sh, .titan, .gitignore.
  2. Grep the repo for any literal "protocol2" and update to "titancall" (README, scripts, comments).

  === B. titancall's own wiring (do NOT touch ~/.local/share/titancall or ~/.config/titancall/config) ===
  3. PATH symlink: `~/.local/bin/titancall` is a symlink pointing at
     `.../projects/common/protocol2/bin/titancall`. Re-point it to the new
     `.../projects/common/titancall/bin/titancall`.
  4. Registry: `~/.config/titancall/registry` is a TSV (titan<TAB>project_path<TAB>last_fired).
     One row's project_path is the old `.../protocol2` dir — change it to the new `.../titancall`
     dir so the project keeps its pinned voice ("ion", set in ./.titan).

  === C. Claude Code per-project state (back up first!) ===
  Claude Code keys project state by a SANITIZED absolute path (every non-alphanumeric char -> "-").
  The old key is:   -Users-d-edens-lab-madness-interactive-projects-common-protocol2
  The new key is:   -Users-d-edens-lab-madness-interactive-projects-common-titancall
  (Don't trust my spelling — LIST the actual dirs and confirm the exact old name before moving.)

  5. Chat history + memory dir: under `~/.claude/projects/`, find the dir ending in
     "...-protocol2" and rename it to the matching "...-titancall" name. This carries the session
     transcripts and the memory/ subdir. If a "...-titancall" dir already exists (a session may
     have created it), MERGE the contents rather than overwrite.
  6. `~/.claude.json` (large JSON, has a top-level "projects" object keyed by ABSOLUTE path).
     Back it up first (`cp ~/.claude.json ~/.claude.json.bak`). Using python or jq (surgical key
     rename — do NOT reformat or rewrite the whole file by hand), rename the key
     "/Users/d.edens/lab/madness_interactive/projects/common/protocol2" to
     "/Users/d.edens/lab/madness_interactive/projects/common/titancall", preserving its value.
     If both keys exist, keep the protocol2 value (it has the real history) under the new key.

  === D. Verify ===
  7. From inside the renamed dir: `titancall whoami` (expect: ion, via dotfile),
     `bash -n bin/titancall` (syntax OK), `titancall titan list` (lists 10 voices incl. portal).
  8. Confirm the symlink resolves: `readlink ~/.local/bin/titancall` points into .../titancall/.
  9. Report every file/path you changed.

  CAVEATS:
  - Do NOT rename the binary, the config dir (~/.config/titancall), or the sounds dir
    (~/.local/share/titancall) — only the project repo dir and references TO it.
  - A live Claude session is currently open in the old protocol2 dir; it may recreate the old
    history path after you move it. Note this in your report so the user knows to reopen Claude in
    the new titancall dir (and re-merge if the old dir reappears).
  - ~/.claude/settings.json hooks reference the SYMLINK (~/.local/bin/titancall), not the repo
    path, so they need no change — but confirm there are no literal "protocol2" paths in it.

  ---
  One honest caveat I can't engineer around: this very session is live in the protocol2 dir. Whatever agent does the history move, when this session next writes (or on exit), it may recreate ...-protocol2.
  Cleanest sequence: let the agent do A–C, then you reopen Claude in the new titancall/ dir and have it merge any leftover ...-protocol2 history that this session flushed. Want me to add an explicit "run
  last / after the user reopens" note, or is the caveat in the prompt enough?
